producer_note: Delas Frères, founded 1835 in the northern Rhône, owned by Louis Roederer (Rouzaud family) since 1993. Produces wines across Hermitage, Crozes-Hermitage, Côte-Rôtie, Cornas, St. Joseph, Condrieu and Côtes du Rhône.

Founded 1977 by Vittorio Moretti in Erbusco. Spans 190 hectares across 10 communes of Franciacorta DOCG, producing traditional-method sparkling wines from Chardonnay and Pinot Noir. Led today by Francesca Moretti.

Founded 1984 by Rita Tua and Virgilio Bisti in Suvereto, Livorno. 60 hectares producing IGT Toscana wines. Flagship Redigaffi (100% Merlot) was first Italian wine to earn Robert Parker 100 points (2000 vintage).

VDP estate based in Schweigen at the French border, with vineyards on both sides (50% in Alsace). Renowned for Pinot Noir/Spätburgunder, with Grosses Gewächs from Kammerberg, Sankt Paul and Sonnenberg vineyards.
Founded 1974 by Pierre and Mireille Giraud. 17 hectares across 64 parcels in Châteauneuf-du-Pape, including old-vine Grenache in La Crau. 100% organic since 2008, biodynamic certified from 2011.
Founded 1858 in Reims by Alexandre Pommery and Narcisse Greno; developed by Louise Pommery, who pioneered Brut Champagne in 1874. Owned by Vranken-Pommery.
Founded 1901 by Don Leoncio Arizu. Family-owned with 700+ ha across seven vineyards in Lujan de Cuyo, Maipu and Valle de Uco. First winery to achieve Lujan de Cuyo DOC (1989).
Joint project of winemaker Alejandro Vigil and historian Adrianna Catena. Sources from Gualtallary (Tupungato) and Agrelo (Lujan de Cuyo); flagship Malbec/Cabernet Franc bottlings.
Established 2016 in Meursault by Pierre-Vincent Girardin (son of Vincent Girardin), 13th-generation vigneron. First vintage 2017. Wines span Cote de Beaune and Cote de Nuits.
Founded 1820 by William and John Graham; owned by Symington Family Estates since 1970. Operates four quintas (Malvedos, Tua, Vila Velha, Vale de Malhadas) totaling 201 ha across the Upper Douro.
Tenuta di Trinoro founded by Andrea Franchetti, who purchased the estate in the 1980s and planted vines in early 1990s near Sarteano. 24 ha of Bordeaux varieties at 450-600m elevation in Val d'Orcia.
Grower-producer in Le Mesnil-sur-Oger since 1889; first estate-bottled cuvee 1928. 8 ha of Grand Cru Chardonnay in Le Mesnil including parcels in Les Chetillons.
Historic Hawke's Bay producer with first vineyards planted 1897 in Taradale, Napier. Owned by Pernod Ricard since 2005. Specializes in Bordeaux blends, Syrah and Chardonnay.

Founded 1830 at Regaleali (550 ha, central Sicily). Eight-generation family producer with five estates: Regaleali, Capofaro (Salina), Tascante (Etna), Whitaker (Mozia) and Sallier de La Tour (Monreale).
Chilean operations centered on Puente Alto in upper Maipo. Co-founded Almaviva with Concha y Toro in 1996/97 (winery built 1998). Cabernet Sauvignon-led Bordeaux blends.

Grower domaine founded 1847 in Ludes; 5th-generation brothers Raphael and Vincent Bereche. 11.5 ha across Ludes and Ormes (Premier Cru Montagne de Reims) and Mareuil-le-Port (Vallee de la Marne). Organic since 2003.
Grower in Les Riceys (Aube/Cote des Bar) on Kimmeridgian limestone. Elise took over family estate in 2008, ~15,000 bottles, biodynamic, all Brut Nature.
Grand Cru grower founded 1870 in Ambonnay (Montagne de Reims). Benoit Marguet took over 2005; 10 ha biodynamic (certified 2008), Extra Brut/Brut Nature dosage.
Grower in Chatillon-sur-Marne (Vallee de la Marne) by 10th-generation vigneron Fabien Caze; first bottling 2013. 4.5 ha organic since 2018, vineyards in Chatillon-sur-Marne and Vandieres.
Hilltop estate in southern Chianti's Valdarno (between Florence and Arezzo). Only winery in the world cultivating Caberlot (a Cabernet Franc/Merlot cross). ~2,000 magnums of Il Caberlot annually.
Founded by brothers Georges and Daniel Daou. Estate is a 100-acre vineyard at 2,200 ft on DAOU Mountain in the Adelaida District AVA of west Paso Robles. Calcareous clay soils; Bordeaux varieties.
Grower-oenologist in Rouvres-les-Vignes (Aube/Cote des Bar). Took over family estate 1993; 3.2 ha of old Pinot Meunier, Pinot Noir, Chardonnay; ~30,000 bottles, solera since 2008.
Founded 1894 in Avize. 10 ha of Chardonnay across Grand Crus Avize, Cramant, Oiry and Oger. Currently led by Pascal Agrapart with son Ambroise; bottling without fining/filtration.
Founded 1972 in Fuenmayor (Rioja Alta); name derives from Logrono-Alava-Navarra. Owned by Sogrape since 2012. ~3.5 million bottles annually with 50,000-barrel cellar.

Founded 1811 in Epernay by Pierre-Nicolas Perrier and Rose-Adelaide Jouet; Belle Epoque prestige cuvee. 266 acres of vineyards across Champagne; Chardonnay-led floral house style. Owned by Pernod Ricard.
Grand Cru grower-producer in Ambonnay (Montagne de Reims), led by 4th-generation Francis Egly. Pinot Noir-focused with cult-status Blanc de Noirs Vieilles Vignes from Ambonnay's chalk and limestone.
Founded early 2000s by Marc de Grazia on Etna's northern slopes. ~43 ha of high-altitude volcanic vineyards across nine Etna DOC contrade; Nerello Mascalese, Nerello Cappuccio and Carricante.
Founded 1928 by Victor Huet in Vouvray; first vineyard Le Haut-Lieu. Benchmark Chenin Blanc producer; biodynamic since 1990, certified 1993. 35 ha; owned by the Hwang family since 2003.

Rallo family producer with five Sicilian estates: Marsala (historic baglio), Contessa Entellina (founding estate), Pantelleria (Zibibbo), Etna (Randazzo) and Vittoria. Native and international varieties.

Family-owned estate in Asoke Valley adjacent to Khao Yai National Park; first vines planted 1999. 40-acre vineyard producing ~90,000 bottles/year. Winemaker Nikki Lohitnavy, Thailand's first qualified winemaker.
Founded 1989 by Alejandro Fernandez (of Tinto Pesquera fame) in Roa, Burgos. ~200 ha of Tempranillo on gravel/clay over chalk along the Duero River; 100% Tempranillo wines aged in American oak.
VDP estate founded 1870 by Balthasar Ress in Hattenheim. ~45 ha, 90% Riesling; also Pinot Blanc and Pinot Noir. Fully organic since 2016 under Christian Ress.

Founded 1973 by Denis and Tricia Horgan in Margaret River, WA. Iconic Art Series Chardonnay (first vintage 1980) is one of Australia's benchmark whites; Art Series spans Chardonnay, Cabernet, Shiraz, Riesling, Sauvignon Blanc.
Cooperative-style house founded 1947 by seven Premier and Grand Cru growers; based in Reims. Sources from 350 growers and 4,000+ ha; 200+ ha of Grand/Premier Cru in Montagne de Reims.
Family producer founded 1896 by Eugenio Collavini, headquartered in Corno di Rosazzo. 170 ha; ~1.5M bottles. Pioneer of Ribolla Gialla sparkling using the proprietary Collavini Method.

Founded 2006 by Sparky and Sarah Marquis in McLaren Vale; estate on Seaview Ridge purchased 2007. Bold, fruit-forward Shiraz-led wines rated by 'Marquis Fruit Weight'. Sarah Marquis took sole ownership in 2017.

Vina Vik founded 2006 by Norwegian Alexander Vik and wife Carrie; 4,300-ha property in Millahue Valley (Cachapoal). Winery completed 2014. Bordeaux-style blends from Carmenere, Cab Sauv, Merlot, Cab Franc, Syrah.
